# attn.markets docs

> Documentation for attn and attn Credit: the credit and servicing layer for agent commerce. Use these pages for product concepts, non-technical mechanics, controls, risk policy, roadmap, and deeper capital-side design where relevant.

These docs describe product mechanics and policy. They are not legal, tax, or investment advice.
These docs currently describe two public product surfaces:
- agent credit for approved Tempo services and approved Virtuals jobs
- borrower credit backed by Pump.fun creator fees

The framing to keep in mind is:
- onchain revenues are proof that the agent already does useful work
- reputation helps shape the starter line before enough repayment history exists
- spend stays bounded to approved rails while the public product remains narrow

Current public proof is still narrow. Hosted agent credit is proven on approved Tempo services and approved Virtuals jobs. Pump borrower remains the strongest borrower-side proof. XLayer should still be read as more tightly bounded where stated.

## Start here
- [Overview](https://docs.attn.markets/): entrypoint and navigation
- [attn 1-pager](https://docs.attn.markets/1-pager): concise product overview
- [Agent credit guide](https://attn.markets/skill.md): how an agent gets small attn credit after one Matrica check to pay for approved services or jobs
- [Borrower estimate](https://www.attn.markets/credit-simulation): estimate flow for credit backed by Pump.fun creator fees
- [The missing layer is credit](https://docs.attn.markets/introduction/the-missing-layer-for-onchain-revenues)
- [How attn Credit works (non-technical)](https://docs.attn.markets/mechanics/how-it-works-nontechnical)
- [Partner-managed wallet integration requirements](https://docs.attn.markets/mechanics/partner-wallet-integration-requirements)
- [attn in context](https://docs.attn.markets/introduction/attn-in-context)
- [Who attn is for](https://docs.attn.markets/introduction/who-attn-is-for)
